page: The UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL40S is a KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture with 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory performance is robust, with large-block read and write bandwidths landing in the top 25%. In LLM inference tests, the server achieves top-tier speeds for small models (135M) and strong text generation speeds for medium models (7B), though performance scales down to average tiers for larger 70B models.

Given the high resource density of 192 GB RAM paired with a 48 GB GPU, this server offers a specialized hardware profile for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated tasks. The absence of local storage requires external storage attachments, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly suitable for machine learning workloads, including small-to-medium LLM inference, image processing, and data science tasks, as well as database operations and high-throughput web serving.
description: The UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL40S is a KVM-based virtual server designed for GPU-accelerated and memory-intensive workloads. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 16 vCPUs (16 physical cores, x86_64 architecture) running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM. Graphics and parallel processing are driven by a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM on the Ada Lovelace architecture. This instance does not include local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside excellent large-block memory bandwidth. It is highly effective for small-to-medium LLM inference, image processing, database operations, and general-purpose GPU computing.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL40S is a KVM virtual server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for GPU and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL40S is a KVM server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU, built for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A high-memory virtual server featuring dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace graphics acceleration for demanding machine learning and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 16 vCPUs and 192 GB of system memory
NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for accelerated parallel computing
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong multi-core benchmark performance
High-capacity memory configuration with 12 GB of RAM per core
No local storage included, requiring external storage solutions
Well-suited for LLM inference, image processing, and database operations
